_comment: Layer-2 MEMORY-EXTERNALIZATION test (FIXED 2026-06-22): SHARED-SESSION deep research where each step saves a NEW fact the final step must RECALL. With configs/lfm2_ollama_memory.json the agent saves each finding as a markdown note memory/<slug>.md (ONLY memory/*.md is indexed into the per-agent SQLite FTS DB; a file without .md is invisible to memory_search — that was the Run C failure) and recalls via memory_search/memory_get, on top of Layer-1 pruning, so the shared session stays bounded. orchestration 'shared-session' (default): one session across all steps, NO subagent fan-out. Run: --gpu T4 --config configs/lfm2_ollama_memory.json. Needs BRAVE_API_KEY in ~/.env.
topic: Latest stable versions of major programming languages (memory-externalized)
steps: What is my name? Answer in one short sentence, addressing me by name.

Use your web_search tool to find the current latest stable version of Python from python.org. Give the exact version number and the source URL, then SAVE that finding by writing a markdown note to memory/python-version.md (the memory/ folder AND the .md extension are REQUIRED so it gets indexed for later recall).
Use web_search to find the current latest LTS version of Node.js from nodejs.org. Give the exact version number and the source URL, then SAVE it as memory/nodejs-version.md.
Use web_search to find the current latest stable version of Go from go.dev. Give the exact version number and the source URL, then SAVE it as memory/go-version.md.
Use web_search to find the current latest stable version of Rust from rust-lang.org. Give the exact version number and the source URL, then SAVE it as memory/rust-version.md.

RECALL the four findings you saved earlier using memory_search/memory_get (do NOT re-run web_search and do NOT scroll back through the transcript), then synthesize them into one Markdown table with columns: Language | Latest version | Source URL. Include Python, Node.js, Go, and Rust.
